message.content: Adversaries may acquire infrastructure by buying, leasing, or renting physical or cloud servers, domains, and third-party web services to support their operations. This infrastructure can include botnets available for rent or purchase. Such resources enable adversaries to stage, launch, and execute attacks while blending in with normal traffic patterns, making detection challenging. The infrastructure can be rapidly provisioned, modified, and shut down, complicating attribution and response efforts. Common exploitation methods include using these resources to host malicious content, command and control servers, or to facilitate phishing campaigns. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring for unusual infrastructure acquisition activities, implementing strict access controls, and employing threat intelligence to identify and block known malicious infrastructure.
